tossups.question: An artist from this country painted Bruno of Cologne witnessing meat turning into ash in front of Carthusian monks. Another painter from this country depicted a bearded woman breastfeeding her baby next to a stone slab. Strings suspending food in windowsills appear in a genre of paintings from this country such as Quince, Cabbage, Melon and Cucumber. Another artist from this country painted The Young Beggar and Children Eating Grapes and a Melon. Cupid holds a pink ribbon and a mirror in front of the title goddess in a painting from this country. A girl steps on a dog on the left of a group portrait from this country around the Infanta Margarita. The Rokeby Venus and Las Meninas were painted in, for 10 points, what home country of Diego Velázquez?
tossups.question_sanitized: An artist from this country painted Bruno of Cologne witnessing meat turning into ash in front of Carthusian monks. Another painter from this country depicted a bearded woman breastfeeding her baby next to a stone slab. Strings suspending food in windowsills appear in a genre of paintings from this country such as Quince, Cabbage, Melon and Cucumber. Another artist from this country painted The Young Beggar and Children Eating Grapes and a Melon. Cupid holds a pink ribbon and a mirror in front of the title goddess in a painting from this country. A girl steps on a dog on the left of a group portrait from this country around the Infanta Margarita. The Rokeby Venus and Las Meninas were painted in, for 10 points, what home country of Diego Velazquez?
tossups.answer: Spain [The opening clues refer to Francisco de Zurbarán, Jusepe de Ribera, bodegón paintings by Juan Sánchez Cotán, and Bartolomé Esteban Murillo, respectively.]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Spain [The opening clues refer to Francisco de Zurbaran, Jusepe de Ribera, bodegon paintings by Juan Sanchez Cotan, and Bartolome Esteban Murillo, respectively.]

tossups.question: This artist created the most widely circulated portrait of the patron saint of the Internet. A woman in a green dress and red cloak holds a stalk of wheat and two urns in this artist’s depiction of St. Rufina. He’s not Caravaggio, but this artist painted a personification of summer in his Young Man with a Basket of Fruit. A portrait attributed to this artist features an engraving of concentric circles on the walls of its setting that represents (*) the heliocentric model. This artist died months after he fell from scaffolding while painting a fresco at a church in Cadiz. A depiction of Mary in a blue cloak surrounded by cherubs is included among this painter’s many depictions of the Immaculate Conception. For 10 points, name this Spanish Baroque painter of Boys Eating Grapes with Melon.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist created the most widely circulated portrait of the patron saint of the Internet. A woman in a green dress and red cloak holds a stalk of wheat and two urns in this artist's depiction of St. Rufina. He's not Caravaggio, but this artist painted a personification of summer in his Young Man with a Basket of Fruit. A portrait attributed to this artist features an engraving of concentric circles on the walls of its setting that represents (*) the heliocentric model. This artist died months after he fell from scaffolding while painting a fresco at a church in Cadiz. A depiction of Mary in a blue cloak surrounded by cherubs is included among this painter's many depictions of the Immaculate Conception. For 10 points, name this Spanish Baroque painter of Boys Eating Grapes with Melon.

tossups.question: A painter from this country, who created a still life of a plate of lemons, a basket of oranges, and a rose next to a cup of water, is often contrasted with a “luminous” painter from here who created many versions of the Immaculate Conception. A tenebrist painter from this country created the Apotheosis of Thomas Aquinas and many paintings of Carthusian monks. In a large painting from this country, the heads of three figures form the lower half of a circle with the head of a kneeling girl wearing a (*) butterfly pin, and also form an X with a dog and the foot of a frame. A history painting from this country is dominated by halberds on its left and pikes on its right, and shows two men embracing and exchanging the key to the title city. The artist stares at you while painting a portrait of a princess from this country and her ladies-in-waiting. For 10 points, name this home country of Diego Velázquez.
tossups.answer: Kingdom of Spain [or Reino de España] (the other painters are Bartolomé Esteban Murillo and Zurbarán; the Velazquez paintings are Las Meninas and The Surrender of Breda)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Kingdom of Spain [or Reino de Espana] (the other painters are Bartolome Esteban Murillo and Zurbaran; the Velazquez paintings are Las Meninas and The Surrender of Breda)

A history painting from this country is dominated by halberds on its left and pikes on its right, and shows two men embracing and exchanging the key to the title city. The artist stares at you while painting a portrait of a princess from this country and her ladies-in-waiting. For 10 points, name this home country of Diego Velazquez.

tossups.question: A painter with this surname is known for his canvases of formless scribbles with words like “yuka” and “burrito” written on them. Another painter with this surname included cherubs struggling to hold up a cloud at the bottom of a painting nicknamed “The Colossal,” which unusually features a full moon at the bottom. A painter of this surname showed a woman resting her chin on her hand next to a woman covering her mouth with her veil in his painting (*) Two Women at a Window. That 17th-century artist of this surname is known for his paintings of the Immaculate Conception, as well as genre scenes like The Young Beggar and Boys Eating Grapes and Melon. For 10 points, identify this surname of the Spanish Baroque painter Bartolomé Esteban.
tossups.question_sanitized: A painter with this surname is known for his canvases of formless scribbles with words like "yuka" and "burrito" written on them. Another painter with this surname included cherubs struggling to hold up a cloud at the bottom of a painting nicknamed "The Colossal," which unusually features a full moon at the bottom. A painter of this surname showed a woman resting her chin on her hand next to a woman covering her mouth with her veil in his painting (*) Two Women at a Window. That 17th-century artist of this surname is known for his paintings of the Immaculate Conception, as well as genre scenes like The Young Beggar and Boys Eating Grapes and Melon. For 10 points, identify this surname of the Spanish Baroque painter Bartolome Esteban.

tossups.question: In a bizarre painting by this man, a leering woman invitingly lifts her veil next to a stone-cold somber bespectacled woman holding the head of a boy whose butt is exposed to the viewer. In an indoor religious painting by this man, this artist depicted a man prostrate on his left knee, holding his hands out at throng of angels emerging from a cloud. Late in his career, this artist cultivated a diffuse "vaporous style," exemplified by the aforementioned Vision of St. Anthony and by his unfinished rendition of The Heavenly and Earthly Trinities for a Capuchin church. Two poor children (*) sumptuously devour grapes and melons in this artist's best known genre-scene. A flirtatious woman at a window covers her laugh with her mantilla shawl in this artist's painting A Girl and Her Duena, one of his many depictions of Galician-born prostitutes in Madrid. For 10 points, name this lighthearted Spanish baroque artist, born a generation after Ribera and Zurbaran.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a bizarre painting by this man, a leering woman invitingly lifts her veil next to a stone-cold somber bespectacled woman holding the head of a boy whose butt is exposed to the viewer. In an indoor religious painting by this man, this artist depicted a man prostrate on his left knee, holding his hands out at throng of angels emerging from a cloud. Late in his career, this artist cultivated a diffuse "vaporous style," exemplified by the aforementioned Vision of St. Anthony and by his unfinished rendition of The Heavenly and Earthly Trinities for a Capuchin church. Two poor children (*) sumptuously devour grapes and melons in this artist's best known genre-scene. A flirtatious woman at a window covers her laugh with her mantilla shawl in this artist's painting A Girl and Her Duena, one of his many depictions of Galician-born prostitutes in Madrid. For 10 points, name this lighthearted Spanish baroque artist, born a generation after Ribera and Zurbaran.

bonuses.leadin: This artist’s The Immaculate Conception of Los Venerables was possibly the most expensive painting in the world when bought by the Louvre from the estate of Marshal Soult For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Baroque artist, noted for their many depictions of the Immaculate Conception. This artist’s works depicting contemporary life include The Young Beggar and The Little Fruit Seller.
Murillo was influenced by this artist, the leading light of Philip IV’s court. The only surviving nude by this artist of The Surrender of Breda is the Rokeby Venus.

Both Murillo and Velázquez came from this city. Velázquez created three versions of a painting titled for a Waterseller of this city, while Murillo painted a St Isidore for this city’s cathedral, one of the largest in the world.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artist's The Immaculate Conception of Los Venerables was possibly the most expensive painting in the world when bought by the Louvre from the estate of Marshal Soult For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Baroque artist, noted for their many depictions of the Immaculate Conception. This artist's works depicting contemporary life include The Young Beggar and The Little Fruit Seller.
Murillo was influenced by this artist, the leading light of Philip IV's court. The only surviving nude by this artist of The Surrender of Breda is the Rokeby Venus.

Both Murillo and Velazquez came from this city. Velazquez created three versions of a painting titled for a Waterseller of this city, while Murillo painted a St Isidore for this city's cathedral, one of the largest in the world.

bonuses.leadin: A stairway, a mirror, and a single star appear in the gaps of the cloud cover in a painting of this subject by Francisco de Zurbarán. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this religious subject that Western artists struggled to depict until the mid-17th century, when the Spanish painter Francisco de Pacheco codified an iconography showing a teenage girl standing on a translucent crescent moon.

This son-in-law of Pacheco painted Mary wearing a bronze tunic in his best-known version of the Immaculate Conception. Columns of lances dominate the upper right of this man’s painting The Surrender of Breda.
References to the Marian litanies are absent from the nearly twenty versions of the Immaculate Conception created by this artist, who painted the beggars and street urchins of his native Seville.
answers: Diego Velázquez [or Diego Rodríguez de Silva y Velázquez]
answers_sanitized: Diego Velazquez [or Diego Rodriguez de Silva y Velazquez]

bonuses.leadin: During the counter-reformation depicting the Immaculate Conception of Mary was doctrinally popular, but also unsurprisingly difficult to draw. For 10 points each:
parts: One model for the Immaculate Conception was the pregnant woman in Revelation 12. This painter of Las Meninas and the Rokeby Venus, acknowledged such a borrowing in his Immaculate Conception by pairing it with a painting of St. John on Patmos.

This Seville born painter and contemporary of Velasquez is perhaps the best known for his paintings of the Immaculate Conception but he painted less luminous scenes in his early tenebrist paintings The Young Beggar and Girl of Galicia.

In Tiepolo’s Immaculate Conception, one of these animals, representing the Holy Ghost, was painted above Mary’s head. One of these animals is also above Jesus’ head in Piero della Francesca’s Baptism of Christ.

bonuses.leadin: Protests against this politician in 2018 began in response to his inadequate handling of the Indio Maíz (mah-EEZ) Biological Reserve forest fires. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Nicaraguan president whose IMF-recommended social security cuts sparked an expansion of the Indio Máiz protests. The protests turned deadly after this man ordered the army to quell them with live ammunition.
Ortega is a member of this socialist party that overthrew Anastasio Somoza in 1979. A Reagan administration scandal arose from illegal funding of the Nicaraguan Contras in order to topple this party.

This Vice President and First Lady of Nicaragua compared the protestors to “vampires in search of blood.” This woman’s political rise began when she defended Ortega from allegations that he had sexually abused her daughter.
answers: Sandinistas [or Sandinista National Liberation Front or FSLN or Frente Sandinista de Liberación Nacional]
Rosario Murillo Zambrana [prompt on Rosario; prompt on “La Chayo”]

bonuses.leadin: Genre paintings by this artist include one of two women at a window, in which one of the women hides her mouth beneath her shawl as if to conceal her snickering. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter who died after falling from scaffolding while working on his Mystic Marriage of St. Catherine for a Capuchin church. He also painted two beggar boys eating grapes and a melon.

Murillo hailed from, and spent most of his career in, this Andalusian city, painting many pictures of its poor. Murillo also painted this city's St Isidore reading from a book, and Diego Velázquez painted a water carrier from here.
Critics use this term for the soft tones and colors of Murillo's religious paintings, such as his Immaculate Conception, which features putti blending into the background like haze.

packet.name: 11 - Theoretical Expenditures of the Leisure Class

bonuses.leadin: In this man’s version of the Immaculate Conception, Mary stands atop a pile of angels. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter also known for his depictions of children and urchins, such as The Young Beggar.
Murillo was a contemporary of this other Spanish painter, who worked at the court of Philip IV and painted Las Meninas and Los Borrachos.
Inspired by a visit to Italy, this historical Velazquez work depicts Justinus van Nassau handing some keys to Ambrogio Spinola after a battle of the 80-Years War.
answers_sanitized: The Surrender of Breda or La rendicion de Breda
